title: CCRG Citation Audit: Chanin NeurIPS-2025 Fix, Muchane Resolved, Winnicki Contrast
layman_summary: Checks that the paper's references point to the right conferences and confirms its three new ideas are genuinely new, fixing one wrong publication year and tracking down a citation that had gone missing.
summary: Closes the iteration-3 M8 novelty/citation minors for the two-track CCRG paper (pure web research). THREE DELIVERABLES. (A) A drop-in Winnicki-2026 contrast: arXiv:2604.23829 (Winnicki, Gnanasekaran, Darve; Stanford ICME; arXiv preprint, no venue) builds feature-level KG edges PURELY OBSERVATIONALLY (corpus co-occurrence graph weighted by Jaccard over binary presence matrices + transcoder cross-layer mechanism graph of source->target sparse latent pathways); a full-text grep finds ZERO occurrences of 'interventional/counterfactual/intervention', so it provably cannot draw CCRG's interventional anchor-205 -> absorber-3069('list') edge (firing-Jaccard<0.1, never co-fire) nor the taxonomic 3792 -> Georgia/Jordan edges (diagnostic KG-agreement 0.318 vs 0.002 null; Jordan 0.99). 2-3 sentence + long paragraph versions provided. (B) Corrected citation table. CRITICAL FIX: Chanin 'A is for Absorption' (2409.14507) = NeurIPS 2025 ORAL (Dec 4 2025; forum R73ybUciQF; 6 authors incl. Golechha), NOT the dossier's 'NeurIPS 2024' (which is the separate NeurIPS-2024 Workshop version, forum Wzav8fesTL, 5 authors, '...in Spelling Tasks'). Muchane2025 RESOLVES to arXiv:2506.01197 (Muchane/Richardson/Park/Veitch) -- no removal needed. Benchmark audit 2605.18229 is SOLE-authored by David Chanin (key 'Chanin2026' VALID). SASA 2606.06333 confirmed (Dalili & Mahdavi). AxBench 2501.17148 = ICML 2025 (not ICLR); SAEBench 2503.09532 = ICML 2025; CanonicalUnits 2502.04878 = ICLR 2025; MindTheGAP 2403.09869 = AISTATS 2024. DPE 2505.23027 and SCR/TPP 2411.18895 have NO venue in arXiv metadata -> cite as preprints. SparseCoactivation 2506.18141 title is now '...Causal Semantic Modules...' (was 'Composable'). Full table + corrections diff + BibTeX in research_report.md. (C) Three-axis novelty confirmation -- interventional co-response grouping, set-cover-for-SAE-grouping, a-priori firing-structure router -- ALL HOLD, with cite-and-distinguish one-liners for the four near-misses (Winnicki 2604.23829; Deng 2506.18141 observational coactivation; CDLC 2505.07073 vision/diffusion analog; Kantamneni 2502.16681 post-hoc, not a-priori). Outputs: research_out.json {answer, sources(19), follow_up_questions(5)} + research_report.md.
answer:
This audit closes the iteration-3 M8 novelty/citation minors for the two-track Counterfactual Co-Response Grouping (CCRG) paper. All work is pure web research; no bibliographic field was invented, and every claim is traceable to a fetched URL. The single highest-risk citation flagged in scoping (Muchane2025) RESOLVED, and the dependency dossier's one known error (Chanin = 'NeurIPS 2024') is corrected.

=== DELIVERABLE A: WINNICKI 2026 CONTRAST ===
Winnicki et al., 'Domain-Filtered Knowledge Graphs from Sparse Autoencoder Features' (arXiv:2604.23829; John Winnicki, Abeynaya Gnanasekaran, Eric Darve, all Institute for Computational and Mathematical Engineering, Stanford University; submitted 26 Apr 2026 v1 / 28 Apr 2026 v2; no venue listed) builds feature-level KG edges from three PURELY OBSERVATIONAL sources [1][2]: (i) a corpus CO-OCCURRENCE graph whose weights are Jaccard similarities J_ab = C_ab/(C_aa+C_bb-C_ab) computed over binary feature-PRESENCE matrices at multiple granularities ('No interventional signals are used -- edges reflect naturalistic co-activation patterns only'); (ii) a TRANSCODER-based cross-layer MECHANISM graph in which each source->target edge is 'a projection of one or more sparse latents that read from the source-side residual stream and write to the target-side residual stream' ('No counterfactual or interventional content manipulation is described'); and (iii) contrastive domain filtering + automated edge labeling. A full-text regex grep of the 90k-char HTML for 'interventional|counterfactual|intervention' returns ZERO matches [2], confirming the pipeline is observational/mechanistic end-to-end. IMPORTANT for honesty: both Winnicki and CCRG emit a feature-level KG, so the distinction is the EDGE SEMANTICS, not the existence of a KG.

Drafted 2-3 sentence contrast (drop-in for Related Work): 'The closest contemporaneous SAE-to-knowledge-graph method, Domain-Filtered Knowledge Graphs (Winnicki et al., 2026), builds its feature-level edges purely observationally -- a corpus co-occurrence graph whose weights are Jaccard overlaps of binary feature-presence matrices, plus a transcoder-based cross-layer mechanism graph linking source- and target-layer latents through sparse pathways -- with no interventional or counterfactual signal anywhere in its pipeline. Such edges cannot, by construction, express CCRG's central relation: on the first-letter-L task CCRG joins anchor latent 205 to absorber latent 3069 (auto-interp label "list"), two latents that are mutually exclusive in firing (firing-Jaccard < 0.1, i.e. they essentially never co-fire), so a Jaccard co-occurrence edge between them is ~0 by definition, decoder geometry need not relate them, and a cross-layer transcoder graph encodes inter-layer pathways rather than within-layer firing-complementarity. CCRG's edge is interventional -- the two latents track the same content counterfactual on disjoint supports (coverage-complementarity recovered by anchored set-cover) -- and the same structure recurs taxonomically, where CCRG recovers an is-a-country anchor (3792) -> Georgia/Jordan specialist edges that an independent, form-free absorption diagnostic corroborates (KG-agreement 0.318 vs a 0.002 null; the Jordan edge agrees at 0.99), specialists an observational co-occurrence graph would again miss.' (A longer single-paragraph version is in research_report.md section A.)

=== DELIVERABLE B: CITATION VENUE/VERSION CORRECTIONS ===
CRITICAL FIX. Chanin et al., 'A is for Absorption: Studying Feature Splitting and Absorption in Sparse Autoencoders' (arXiv:2409.14507) is a NeurIPS 2025 ORAL (oral session Thursday Dec 4 2025, 3:30-3:50 PM PST), main conference, OpenReview forum R73ybUciQF, six authors: David Chanin, James Wilken-Smith, Tomas Dulka, Hardik Bhatnagar, Satvik Golechha, Joseph Isaac Bloom [3][4]. The dependency dossier source [15] mislabels it 'NeurIPS 2024'; all paper text and .bib must read NeurIPS 2025 (Oral). PLAN-NOTE: the plan's assumed forum 'Wzav8fesTL' and 'poster 118058' are slightly off -- Wzav8fesTL is the separate NeurIPS 2024 WORKSHOP on Interpretable AI version (subtitle '...in Spelling Tasks', five authors, no Golechha) [5]; the 2025 main-conference forum is R73ybUciQF, and the definitive presentation type is ORAL (virtual page oral/130303), with the customary companion poster slot (poster/118058).

Muchane2025 RESOLVED (highest-risk citation): 'Incorporating Hierarchical Semantics in Sparse Autoencoder Architectures', arXiv:2506.01197, Mark Muchane, Sean Richardson, Kiho Park, Victor Veitch (submitted 1 Jun 2025; code at github.com/muchanem/hierarchical-sparse-autoencoders; no venue in metadata) -- a modified SAE architecture explicitly modeling a parent/child semantic hierarchy, reporting improvements on interpretability metrics including feature absorption [8]. No removal/replacement needed.

Benchmark audit 2605.18229 ('Are Sparse Autoencoder Benchmarks Reliable?') is SOLE-authored by David Chanin (submitted 18 May 2026; no venue) -- so the artifact-direction key 'Chanin2026' is VALID, and it audits SAEBench finding TPP and SCR fail multiple lenses [7]. SASA 2606.06333 confirmed: 'Subspace-Aware Sparse Autoencoders for Effective Mechanistic Interpretability', Seyed Arshan Dalili & Mehrdad Mahdavi (submitted 4 Jun 2026; no venue); key 'Dalili2026' matches first author [6].

Headline-venue confirmations: AxBench 2501.17148 = ICML 2025 (icml.cc poster 45658), confirming the dossier's 'ICML 2025 (not ICLR)' and refuting a stray third-party 'ICLR Spotlight' claim [9]; SAEBench 2503.09532 = ICML 2025 (PMLR v267; icml.cc poster 43918) [10]; 'Sparse Autoencoders Do Not Find Canonical Units of Analysis' 2502.04878 = ICLR 2025 (iclr.cc poster 30676; OpenReview 9ca9eHNrdH; Leask, Bussmann, Pearce, Bloom, Tigges, Al Moubayed, Sharkey, Nanda) [11]; Mind the GAP 2403.09869 = AISTATS 2024 (Rudner, Zhang, Wilson, Kempe) [15]; Feature Hedging 2505.11756 = arXiv preprint 2025 (Chanin, Dulka, Garriga-Alonso) [12]; Sparse Feature Coactivation 2506.18141 -- current title 'Sparse Feature Coactivation Reveals CAUSAL Semantic Modules in Large Language Models' (v1 said 'Composable'), Deng et al. (UMich), arXiv preprint 2025 [13]. PREPRINT-ONLY (no venue in arXiv metadata; cite as arXiv, do not assert a conference): DPE 2505.23027 (dossier said ICML 2025; not re-confirmed) [14] and the SCR/TPP origin 2411.18895 (third-party listings say NeurIPS 2024; unconfirmed) [16]. Long-settled foundations were sanity-glanced per plan; no change.

=== DELIVERABLE C: THREE-AXIS NOVELTY CONFIRMATION ===
Axis 1 (group SAE latents by INTERVENTIONAL/counterfactual co-response): HOLDS. The nearest works are observational or in another modality -- Winnicki 2604.23829 (co-occurrence + transcoder, observational) [1][2]; Deng 2506.18141 forms modules from observational COACTIVATION across a few prompts then validates causally (grouping signal is observational; CCRG's grouping signal is itself the intervention) [13]; O'Neill 2408.00657 'feature families' (observational); CDLC/Varshney 2505.07073 clusters latent DIFFERENCE vectors from factual/counterfactual IMAGE pairs in diffusion/vision (Pattern Recognition Letters) -- the vision analog, which CCRG instantiates on LLM SAE latents plus the set-cover K-track [17]. No 2024-2026 paper groups LLM SAE latents by content-counterfactual co-response.

Axis 2 (SET-COVER / max-coverage / submodular to GROUP or SELECT SAE features): HOLDS. Searches surfaced only the generic set-cover literature and unrelated SAE work: 'SAE Features for Classifications and Transferability' 2502.11367 (feature SELECTION for probes, not coverage grouping); Group-SAE (EMNLP 2025; groups LAYERS for training efficiency); Model-X Knockoffs for SAE features 2511.11711 (FDR control). The (1-1/e) greedy max-coverage guarantee (NWF 1978; Feige JACM 1998) applied to content-cover sets of SAE latents to recover absorbers appears genuinely novel [18].

Axis 3 (A-PRIORI firing-structure ROUTER predicting WHEN SAE grouping helps): HOLDS. Nearest is Kantamneni/Engels/Rajamanoharan/Tegmark/Nanda 2502.16681 'Are Sparse Autoencoders Useful? A Case Study in Sparse Probing' -- a POST-HOC empirical comparison concluding SAEs offer no consistent advantage, explicitly NOT a predictive diagnostic [19]; and the Chanin absorption metric DETECTS absorption on individual latents post-hoc rather than routing between methods [3][7]. CCRG's firing-Jaccard/mutual-exclusivity router PREDICTS the regime a priori from one forward pass. 2502.16681 also directly supports the paper's motivating premise that simple baselines often beat raw-latent SAE methods.

Overall: all three novelty claims HOLD; the honest recommendation is to cite-and-distinguish the four near-misses (Winnicki 2604.23829, Deng 2506.18141, CDLC 2505.07073, Kantamneni 2502.16681) with the one-line differentiators rather than assert novelty in a vacuum. CONFIDENCE: high for the Chanin fix, Muchane resolution, benchmark-audit authorship, Winnicki observational-edges claim, AxBench/SAEBench (ICML 2025), CanonicalUnits (ICLR 2025), MindTheGAP (AISTATS 2024); medium for DPE/SCR-TPP venues (cite as preprints pending a proceedings page).

sources.title: Domain-Filtered Knowledge Graphs from Sparse Autoencoder Features (Winnicki, Gnanasekaran, Darve)
sources.summary: Abstract/metadata: confirms title, three Stanford authors, submission 26 Apr 2026 (v1)/28 Apr 2026 (v2), no venue (arXiv preprint 2026); edges = co-occurrence graph + transcoder-based mechanism graph + contrastive domain filtering.

sources.title: Winnicki 2026 full HTML — edge construction + affiliation + intervention-term grep
sources.summary: Verbatim: co-occurrence counts from binary presence matrices, Jaccard edge weights, multi-granularity; transcoder source->target readout projections; explicit 'No interventional signals are used' / 'No counterfactual or interventional content manipulation'; all three authors at Stanford ICME; full-text grep for interventional/counterfactual/intervention = 0 matches.

sources.title: NeurIPS 2025 Oral — A is for Absorption (Chanin et al.)
sources.summary: Authoritative venue: NeurIPS 2025, ORAL, session Thu Dec 4 2025 3:30-3:50 PM PST; six authors incl. Satvik Golechha and Joseph Bloom. Corrects dossier's 'NeurIPS 2024'.

sources.title: OpenReview — A is for Absorption (NeurIPS 2025 main conference)
sources.summary: Venue field 'NeurIPS 2025', decision 'NeurIPS 2025 oral', main-conference submission; full 6-author list with spelling/order. This is the correct 2025 forum (not Wzav8fesTL).

sources.title: OpenReview — A is for Absorption (NeurIPS 2024 Workshop variant)
sources.summary: Distinguishes the separate NeurIPS 2024 Workshop on Interpretable AI version: subtitle '...in Spelling Tasks', five authors (no Golechha). Shows the plan's assumed forum was the workshop, not the 2025 main paper.

sources.title: Subspace-Aware Sparse Autoencoders for Effective Mechanistic Interpretability (SASA)
sources.summary: Confirms full title (dossier truncated), authors Seyed Arshan Dalili & Mehrdad Mahdavi, submitted 4 Jun 2026, no venue (arXiv preprint 2026); key 'Dalili2026' matches first author; learned decoder subspaces + block sparsity reduce feature splitting.

sources.title: Are Sparse Autoencoder Benchmarks Reliable? (Chanin)
sources.summary: arXiv abs/HTML grep: SOLE author David Chanin, submitted 18 May 2026, no venue; audits SAEBench and finds TPP and SCR fail multiple lenses. Validates the 'Chanin2026' citation key.

sources.title: Incorporating Hierarchical Semantics in Sparse Autoencoder Architectures (Muchane et al.)
sources.summary: RESOLVES the highest-risk 'Muchane2025' cite: Mark Muchane, Sean Richardson, Kiho Park, Victor Veitch; submitted 1 Jun 2025; code on GitHub; explicit parent/child semantic hierarchy improving reconstruction, interpretability, and feature-absorption metrics. No venue in metadata.

sources.title: ICML 2025 — AxBench (Wu et al.)
sources.summary: Authoritative venue page confirming AxBench (2501.17148) is ICML 2025, not ICLR; AxBench's own finding ('Even Simple Baselines Outperform Sparse Autoencoders') supports the paper's motivation.

sources.title: PMLR v267 (ICML 2025) — SAEBench (Karvonen et al.)
sources.summary: Authoritative venue: SAEBench (2503.09532) is ICML 2025 (PMLR v267); also icml.cc/virtual/2025/poster/43918. Adds the venue the dossier left unspecified.

sources.title: ICLR 2025 — Sparse Autoencoders Do Not Find Canonical Units of Analysis
sources.summary: Confirms 2502.04878 = ICLR 2025 (also OpenReview 9ca9eHNrdH; proceedings.iclr.cc); authors Leask, Bussmann, Pearce, Bloom, Tigges, Al Moubayed, Sharkey, Nanda; SAE stitching (incomplete) + meta-SAEs (non-atomic).

sources.title: Feature Hedging: Correlated Features Break Narrow Sparse Autoencoders (Chanin, Dulka, Garriga-Alonso)
sources.summary: arXiv preprint 2025 (submitted 16 May 2025, rev 26 Sep 2025), no conference venue; narrow SAEs merge correlated features via reconstruction loss.

sources.title: Sparse Feature Coactivation Reveals Causal Semantic Modules in LLMs (Deng et al.)
sources.summary: Title corrected to 'Causal' (v1 said 'Composable'); authors Deng, Hu, Gilberti, Storks, Taxali, Angstadt, Sripada, Chai (UMich); submitted 22 Jun 2025; observational coactivation modules validated causally -- Axis-1 near-miss (observational grouping signal).

sources.title: Diverse Prototypical Ensembles Improve Robustness to Subpopulation Shift (To et al.)
sources.summary: arXiv abs grep: Minh Nguyen Nhat To + 8 authors, submitted 29 May 2025, NO venue in arXiv metadata (dossier's 'ICML 2025' not independently re-confirmed); cite as preprint until proceedings located. Baseline (k) competitor.

sources.title: Mind the GAP: Group-Aware Priors (Rudner, Zhang, Wilson, Kempe)
sources.summary: Confirms AISTATS 2024 (listed on virtual.aistats.org/virtual/2024); NYU + Meta FAIR; group-aware prior favoring high-worst-group params; baseline (j) alternative.

sources.title: Evaluating Sparse Autoencoders on Targeted Concept Erasure Tasks (Karvonen et al.) — SCR/TPP origin
sources.summary: arXiv abs grep: submitted 28 Nov 2024, NO venue in arXiv metadata (third-party listings claim NeurIPS 2024; unconfirmed); origin of SCR (from SHIFT) and TPP. Cite as arXiv preprint.

sources.title: Discovering Concept Directions from Diffusion-based Counterfactuals via Latent Clustering (CDLC, Varshney et al.)
sources.summary: Vision/diffusion analog: clusters latent DIFFERENCE vectors from factual/counterfactual IMAGE pairs (skin-lesion data); Pattern Recognition Letters, ICPR-2024 special section. Axis-1 precedent in a different modality, not an SAE/LLM competitor.

sources.title: Maximum coverage / set-cover (landscape check for Axis 2)
sources.summary: Greedy max-coverage = (1-1/e) approximation; Axis-2 search surfaced only generic set-cover plus unrelated SAE work (2502.11367 feature selection; Group-SAE layer grouping; 2511.11711 knockoffs) — no set-cover-for-SAE-latent-grouping, confirming novelty.

sources.title: Are Sparse Autoencoders Useful? A Case Study in Sparse Probing (Kantamneni, Engels, Rajamanoharan, Tegmark, Nanda)
sources.summary: Axis-3 near-miss: POST-HOC empirical comparison finding SAEs offer no consistent advantage over baselines; explicitly NOT an a-priori predictive router. Also supports the 'simple baselines beat raw SAE latents' premise.

follow_up_questions: Chanin 'A is for Absorption' (2409.14507) is confirmed NeurIPS 2025 ORAL — should the paper additionally cite the separate NeurIPS 2024 Workshop version (forum Wzav8fesTL, '...in Spelling Tasks') as the earlier spelling-specific result, or only the 2025 main-conference paper?

DPE (2505.23027) and the SCR/TPP origin (2411.18895) have no venue in arXiv metadata — should they be cited strictly as arXiv preprints, or should a follow-up locate the ICML-2025 / NeurIPS-2024 camera-ready pages to upgrade the entries?

Muchane 2506.01197, SASA 2606.06333, Winnicki 2604.23829, and Deng 2506.18141 currently show no conference venue — is it worth re-checking closer to submission whether any has since been accepted (e.g., NeurIPS 2026), to upgrade from 'arXiv preprint'?

Deng et al. 2506.18141 changed its title from 'Composable' (v1) to 'Causal' (v2) Semantic Modules — should the bib/related-work pin the current 'Causal' title and note the coactivation-vs-counterfactual grouping-signal distinction explicitly?

CDLC (2505.07073) is the vision/diffusion analog of clustering counterfactual difference vectors — should the paper foreground it as the cross-modal precedent for CCRG's C-track, framing CCRG as the LLM/SAE instantiation with the added set-cover K-track?
